title: Deployment reconciliation preflight (Render blueprint blockers)
objective: Reconcile deployment configuration before any Blueprint provisioning: create services/api/requirements.txt (or align blueprint to the packaging actually used); align health-check path to /api/v1/health; add NEXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL or a same-origin proxy for the Render web service; fix or remove configured worker and cron entrypoints that do not exist; complete auth/CORS/security-header baseline. Do NOT provision the Blueprint (B-002 remains owner-gated)
business_reason: Owner code-audit directive 2026-07-17 deployment-blocker list; prevents a broken first deploy
inputs: Owner code-audit directive 2026-07-17 deployment-blocker list (verbatim in project-control/reports/OWNER-REVIEW-code-audit-task-consolidation.md section 2.4): services/api/requirements.txt missing; health path is /api/v1/health not /health; Render web lacks NEXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL or same-origin proxy; configured worker/cron entrypoints do not exist; auth/CORS/security headers incomplete
render.yaml current blueprint + services/api actual packaging (pyproject-based install)
project-control/reports/M2-T001-G3-review.md D8 (CORS/proxy decision recorded as an explicit pre-deployment blocker)
M0-T006 deployment/rollback procedure records (the runbook this task corrects)
outputs: Blueprint/packaging reconciliation: services/api/requirements.txt created OR render.yaml build command aligned to the packaging actually used (decision documented)
Health-check path aligned to /api/v1/health everywhere (blueprint, runbook, monitors)
Web-service API wiring: NEXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL env var in the blueprint or an implemented same-origin proxy (resolves M2-T001 D8; decision documented)
Worker and cron entrypoints in render.yaml either implemented as real modules or removed until their milestones deliver them (no phantom entrypoints)

API security baseline: CORS origin allowlist middleware, security headers, honest documentation that auth is NOT yet enabled (M0-T007/T008 blocked on B-001) and therefore NO public exposure until it is
Updated deployment runbook (exact file confirmed at G0 from M0-T006 records)
NO provisioning: zero Render API calls; B-002 stays owner-gated
allowed_paths: services/api/** (packaging, health route alias if needed, CORS/security-header middleware only)
docs/ deployment runbook file only (exact path confirmed at G0 from M0-T006 records)
forbidden_paths: .claude/**, .github/** (CI untouched; this is deploy config, not CI)
acceptance_scenarios: S1 blueprint truth: every buildCommand/startCommand/entrypoint in render.yaml resolves to files/modules that exist in the repo (scripted check in the report)
S2 health: the blueprint health-check path matches the live route (/api/v1/health) and the runbook says the same
S3 wiring: web service reaches the API via the chosen mechanism (env var or proxy) with the decision + rationale recorded; .env.example carries names only
S4 CORS/headers: pytest proves disallowed origin rejected, configured origin allowed, security headers present on API responses
S5 honesty: runbook and blueprint state auth is absent and public exposure is blocked until M0-T007/T008; no secrets anywhere; secret scan green
S6 no provisioning: no Render account/API interaction occurred; B-002 unchanged
S7 G5 review passes on the CORS/header/no-auth-exposure posture
risks: Do not provision anything (B-002 owner-gated); this is configuration truth-reconciliation only
CORS/proxy decision affects M2-T002's deployed behavior later - record the decision so the frontend does not hardcode assumptions
